skill.name: grant-gantt-chart-gen
skill.description: Generates project timeline visualizations for grant proposals. Reads milestone data and produces ASCII, Mermaid, or JSON output suitable for inclusion in research grant applications.
skill.summary: Generate Gantt chart timelines for grant proposals from milestone data.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Evaluated 52 static analysis findings: all are false positives except one benign filesystem write. The 6 'weak cryptographic algorithm' hits match JSON score values, argparse strings, and markdown text, not actual crypto. The 34 'shell backtick execution' hits are Markdown code formatting, not Ruby shell execution. The 5 'system reconnaissance' hits are English prose and method names. Path traversal and URL hits are in a security checklist and a source attribution, respectively. The only confirmed pattern is the filesystem write at scripts/main.py:165, which is the legitimate core function of saving generated charts. No malicious intent, prompt injection, or data exfiltration detected. The legacy audit independently grades this skill as Production Ready (85/100) with all veto gates passing.

low_findings.title: Filesystem write operation in main script
low_findings.description: scripts/main.py opens a user-specified output file for writing the generated chart. This is the intended core functionality of the skill. The output path is controlled via the --output CLI argument. No path traversal or unexpected write behavior detected.

content.user_title: Generate Grant Gantt Charts for Research Proposals
content.value_statement: Creating project timelines for grant proposals is time-consuming and difficult to maintain manually. This skill generates structured Gantt chart visualizations from milestone data, helping researchers produce clear project timelines for their grant applications.
actual_capabilities: Generate ASCII Gantt charts from milestone data with task bars and headers
Create Mermaid timeline diagrams for embedding in grant proposal documents
Produce structured JSON timeline data with quarterly breakdowns and milestone dates
Support configurable project duration from 1 to 36 or more months
Generate milestone calendars with date, month offset, and quarter annotations
Output timeline visualizations to a file for inclusion in proposal submissions
limitations: Does not support interactive timeline editing or drag-and-drop adjustments
Cannot import data from project management tools like Microsoft Project or Jira
Limited to ASCII, Mermaid, and JSON output formats only
No built-in dependency tracking for parallel task paths or critical path analysis

use_cases.title: Grant Proposal Timeline Planning
use_cases.description: A researcher preparing an NIH grant proposal needs a 5-year project timeline with specific milestones for each year.

use_cases.title: Department Project Portfolio
use_cases.description: A lab manager needs to visualize multiple research projects and their timelines on a single chart for reporting.

use_cases.title: Progress Report Visualization
use_cases.description: A grant administrator needs to generate updated timeline charts for annual grant progress reports.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Grant Timeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 3-year grant project timeline with milestones at year 1, year 2, and year 3.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user creating a simple grant timeline with default milestones

prompt_templates.title: Custom Milestone Timeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 5-year project timeline with milestones at months 6, 12, 24, 36, 48, and 60. Include quarterly breakdowns.
prompt_templates.scenario: User with specific milestone dates needs a detailed project timeline

prompt_templates.title: Mermaid Chart for Proposal
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Gantt chart for a 4-year research project with 6 tasks. Use Mermaid format so I can embed it in my grant proposal document.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs an embeddable chart format for direct inclusion in a proposal

prompt_templates.title: Custom Project with Multiple Aims
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 36-month project Gantt chart with 4 research aims as tasks. Each aim has different start months and durations. Output in ASCII format and save to file. Start date is 2026-07-01.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user configuring a multi-aim research project with specific start date

output_examples.input: Create a 3-year grant timeline with milestones at months 12, 24, and 36.
output: Task                M1 M2 M3 M4 M5 M6 M7 M8 M9 M10 M11 M12 ...

output_examples.input: Generate a Mermaid gantt chart for a 2-year research project with 3 tasks.

best_practices: Define clear milestones with realistic month offsets before generating the timeline chart
Review the generated timeline for accuracy against your grant application submission guidelines
Use Mermaid output format when embedding timelines in documents for better rendering control
anti_patterns: Avoid using unrealistic task durations that compress or stretch activities beyond feasible timeframes
Do not bypass input validation by providing malformed milestone data that could cause unexpected script behavior
Avoid generating timelines without confirming the project start date and total duration first

faq.question: What output formats does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill supports three output formats: ASCII text for quick review, Mermaid syntax for embedding in documents, and JSON for programmatic use.

faq.question: How do I specify milestones for my project?
faq.answer: Provide a JSON file with milestone objects containing name and month fields, or let the skill use default milestones at 12, 24, and 36 months.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for non-research projects?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill generates Gantt charts for any project timeline, even though it was designed for grant proposals.

faq.question: Does this skill work with existing project management data?
faq.answer: The skill accepts milestone data in JSON format. Data from other tools must be converted to JSON before use.

faq.question: What is the maximum project duration supported?
faq.answer: The duration is configurable with any positive integer. The default is 36 months.

faq.question: Can I customize the task names and durations?
faq.answer: Yes. You can specify custom tasks with name, start month, and duration values when generating the chart.
